title: Multi-regulator breach-notification SLA tracker (24h NIS2 / 72h GDPR / 72h HIPAA)
exclusions: Does NOT determine whether an event rises to 'breach'; relies on classification decided in the incident-management system.
evidence: Saved search 'breach_notification_sla' runs every 15 min and writes per-incident SLA status (against every applicable regulator) to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.33(1) requires notification to the supervisory authority without undue delay and where feasible within 72 hours; the UC measures elapsed time from classification to submission evidence.
NIS2 Art.23 imposes an early-warning obligation within 24h and an incident notification within 72h; the UC evidences both clocks for every eligible incident.
DORA Art.19 requires major ICT-related incident reporting; SLA tracking against intermediate and final reports is the canonical evidence.
§164.308(a)(6) security incident procedures include notification duties per §164.400-414; the UC surfaces the timer but breach assessment is decided by the privacy officer.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.33 (parent assurance='full'). Clause mapped per data/regulations.json derivesFrom graph; inherited assurance degraded one step.

Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.33 (parent assurance='full'). Clause mapped per data/regulations.json derivesFrom graph; inherited assurance degraded one step. Divergence recorded — SME review should confirm scope fit.

derivationSource.divergenceNote: ANPD is the competent authority; notification timelines differ.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.33 (parent assurance='full'). Clause mapped per data/regulations.json derivesFrom graph; inherited assurance degraded one step. Divergence recorded — SME review should confirm scope fit.

derivationSource.divergenceNote: Swiss FDPIC replaces EU supervisory authority for breach notification.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.33 (parent assurance='full'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When an incident is classified 'breach' (per incident_register.csv) and no regulator-submission record exists 90 minutes before the applicable SLA deadline, the UC fires with a warning; at breach, it fires at severity critical.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Incidents not classified as breach, or incidents whose notification was submitted before the deadline with a valid tracking_id, do NOT fire.

spl:
| inputlookup incident_register.csv
| where classification="breach"
| eval applicable_regs=split(applicable_regs,",")
| mvexpand applicable_regs
| lookup sla_catalog.csv regulator as applicable_regs OUTPUT sla_hours
| eval deadline=strptime(classified_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")+sla_hours*3600
| join incident_id applicable_regs [search index=notifications sourcetype=regulator:submission | fields incident_id applicable_regs tracking_id submitted_at]
| eval status=case(isnull(tracking_id) AND now()>deadline, "breach", isnull(tracking_id) AND (deadline-now())<5400, "imminent", isnotnull(tracking_id), "submitted")
| table incident_id applicable_regs deadline submitted_at status
description: Tracks every incident that has crossed the 'breach' threshold and reports on the notification clock per applicable regulator. Imminent breaches trigger a warning; missed deadlines trigger a critical notable and a board-level communication.
value: Most enforcement actions against supervisory breaches (ICO, DPC, BaFin) focus on notification timeliness. This UC turns the obligation into a visible KPI instead of a post-facto audit finding.
implementation: (1) Ingest classifications from ServiceNow / ES incident index; (2) maintain sla_catalog.csv with regulator → SLA hours; (3) ingest notification-portal tracking IDs; (4) schedule every 15 min; (5) integrate with SOAR to auto-escalate.
visualization: Gantt of incidents vs SLA deadlines, single-value 'incidents notified on time (90d)', breakdown by regulator.
knownFalsePositives: Incidents whose submission happens offline with post-hoc upload require a timezone-normalised submitted_at; mis-mapped timezones produce false SLA breaches.
